name: Electric Cooperatives
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving electric cooperative employees and directors with savings accounts, loans, and financial services from Little Rock, Arkansas.
description_long:
Electric Cooperatives Federal Credit Union (ECFCU) is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative headquartered in Little Rock, Arkansas. Established to serve a specific membership base, the credit union operates under the federal credit union charter and is insured by the NCUA. The institution emphasizes sound financial services, professional management, and strong financial stability in its operations.

ECFCU offers a comprehensive range of consumer financial products including savings accounts, checking accounts, health savings accounts (HSAs), and various loan products. Members can access online banking, mobile app services, wire transfers, direct deposit/ACH transfers, and payroll deduction services. The credit union also provides insurance services including credit life and disability insurance through Trustage, and maintains calculators and a resource center for member education.

The credit union differentiates itself through its commitment to community service and member-centric operations. ECFCU partners with Credit Union for Kids in support of Children's Miracle Network for Arkansas Children's Hospital and supports the Cornerstone Credit Union Foundation, which provides scholarships, grants, and disaster relief to credit unions and their employees. As a member-owned cooperative, all profits theoretically benefit members rather than external shareholders.

A significant limitation is membership eligibility—ECFCU is restricted to employees or directors of rural electric cooperatives, making it inaccessible to the general public. The website lacks detailed information on specific loan rates, account fees, APY rates, and minimum balance requirements. Members must contact the credit union directly for pricing information, and the limited online transparency may be inconvenient for prospective members evaluating financial terms.
services: Loan request forms and account management tools
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means potential for better rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks
NCUA insurance protection on accounts up to $250,000
Mobile app available on App Store for convenient banking access
Offers specialized products like Health Savings Accounts (HSAs) for tax-advantaged savings
Credit life and disability insurance available through Trustage for qualifying members
Payroll deduction service for convenient automatic savings and loan payments
Active community involvement with Children's Miracle Network and Cornerstone Credit Union Foundation
cons: Membership restricted to employees or directors of rural electric cooperatives—not available to general public
Limited transparency on website regarding loan rates, account APY, fees, and minimum balance requirements
Single branch location in Little Rock, Arkansas with no indication of ATM network or branch expansion
Incomplete membership eligibility section on website (text appears cut off mid-sentence)
No clear information about loan approval timelines, credit score requirements, or specific loan products offered
best_for: Electric cooperative employees seeking member-owned financial institution benefits and community-focused banking
Rural electric cooperative directors needing comprehensive financial services from a credit union with industry-specific focus
Members wanting to support cooperatives values and community service initiatives through their banking relationship
Employees of qualifying organizations seeking lower-cost alternatives to traditional banks with NCUA protection
diagnosis: Electric Cooperatives FCU is best suited for employees and directors of rural electric cooperatives seeking member-owned banking with competitive rates, community values, and NCUA protection. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—this credit union is inaccessible to the general public and only serves the electric cooperative industry employee base, making it unsuitable for most consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Electric Cooperatives — NCUA-insured credit union in LITTLE ROCK, AR. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
